title: Hortensia scandens
intro: Hortensia scandens, also known as Lonicera, is a type of flowering vine native to Asia, characterized by its fragrant, white, yellow, or pink flowers and purple berries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hortensia scandens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hortensia scandens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in dried flower and berry form, and is commonly used to relieve respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Hortensia scandens is a type of flowering plant commonly known as the honeysuckle vine. It belongs to the family Caprifoliaceae. This family is a part of the order Dipsacales. uses: Hortensia scandens, also known as the climbing hydrangea, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
